title: Brickyard retro one-page menu: food-photo grade + logo vectorize + InDesign data-merge to print PDF/JPEG
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Freelancer.com brief: a "creative, vibrant, and appetizing one-page menu design for a fast-food burger restaurant named Brickyard"; "Retro, street-food style, or rustic chalkboard feel"; sections "Classic Burgers, Specialty Burgers, Sides, and Beverages (Just use placeholder text/prices for now)"; "Must include space for high-quality food photos"; deliverables "High-res JPEG and print-ready PDF." REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-only benchmark: the original brief implies a from-scratch layout, which is NOT in connector scope (no layout composition; render_layout/merge_data_layout only EXPORT/bind a GENUINE authored .indd). So the client is modeled as supplying the authored InDesign menu template carrying real <<fields>> + photo frames (a normal freelancer hand-off / second-round revision scenario), the four raw food photos, the wordmark, the chalkboard texture choice, and the placeholder prices CSV. The Adobe chain then does the genuinely-doable heavy lifting the brief actually rewards: color-grading + cropping the food photos so they look appetizing, vectorizing the wordmark for crisp print, sourcing the rustic chalkboard texture, binding the placeholder copy via real InDesign data-merge, and exporting the print PDF + high-res JPEG. The "placeholder text/prices" instruction is honored literally via the CSV. No deliverable was invented beyond the menu the brief asks for.
one_line_ask: Grade four raw burger photos, vectorize the Brickyard wordmark, source a chalkboard texture, and run the client's authored InDesign menu template through a data-merge to produce a print-ready PDF and high-res JPEG.
full_brief: Brickyard, a retro street-food burger joint, needs its one-page menu finished for print. The owner has handed off an authored InDesign menu template (menu_template.indd) laid out in a rustic chalkboard style with four photo frames and real data-merge fields (<<section>>, <<item>>, <<desc>>, <<price>> across Classic Burgers, Specialty Burgers, Sides, Beverages), the brand wordmark as a flat PNG, four raw phone-shot food photos (a double Brickyard, a specialty bacon-jalapeño burger, a basket of fries, a craft cola), and a CSV of placeholder item names / descriptions / prices. The four food photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull/flat, cool white-balance, busy backgrounds. The job: (1) make every food photo look vibrant and appetizing — give the hero double-smash shot the full grade (straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance toward a hot street-food look, push vibrance, apply a food-pop preset, and throw the cluttered background out of focus so the burger reads as hero), and bring the other three to a matching appetizing look through their own lighter auto-tone / warm-up (plus a background lens-blur where the background is busy) sub-chains; consistently crop all four to the same frame ratio the template expects; (2) lift the hero double-smash shot cleanly onto a transparent background so it can sit over the chalkboard without a hard photographic edge; (3) vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp at large print size; (4) license a rustic chalkboard/blackboard texture from Adobe Stock to back the layout; (5) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the placeholder-prices CSV so each section/item/price binds into the template, and export the bound document to a print-ready PDF and a high-resolution JPEG; (6) assemble the produced assets onto a Firefly board so the owner can sign off on the photo treatment and final menu in one deep-link. Every section/price stays placeholder per the brief.

inputs.name: menu_template.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: AUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for a one-page A4 portrait Brickyard menu in rustic chalkboard style. Must contain four image frames sized to a 4:5 ratio and REAL InDesign data-merge placeholder fields named <<section>>, <<item>>, <<desc>>, <<price>> arranged under four section headers (Classic Burgers, Specialty Burgers, Sides, Beverages), plus a placeholder area for the wordmark and a full-bleed background frame for the chalkboard texture. Authored by hand in InDesign so the data-merge fields actually bind; a PDF-converted .indd with literal text would NOT bind.
inputs.realism_notes: Modeled as the client's own draft template (a normal freelancer revision/hand-off). document_merge_data_layout is [T] and REQUIRES a user-authored .indd carrying real merge fields — it cannot be connector-generated, so it is listed as a supplied input, not a chain output.

inputs.name: photo_classic_double_smash.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone snapshot of a double Brickyard on a wax-paper-lined metal basket, sitting on a cluttered diner table with ketchup bottles and napkins blurred behind it. Slightly tilted horizon (about 4 degrees), flat dull lighting, slightly cool/blue white balance, visible sesame-seed bun texture, melted cheese drip, real grease sheen, no studio gloss. Documentary candid look, slight motion imperfection.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately imperfect (tilt, flat tone, cool WB, busy background) so the grading chain has real work to do; gpt-image-2 for photoreal food.

inputs.name: photo_specialty_bacon_jalapeno.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone snapshot of a tall specialty burger with bacon, jalapeños and a brioche bun on a slate board, harsh overhead cafeteria lighting, dull underexposed shadows, cool white balance, distracting background of a busy kitchen pass. Real food texture, grease, crumb, no CGI gloss.
inputs.realism_notes: Underexposed + busy background to justify auto-tone, warm-up, vibrance and background lens-blur steps.

inputs.name: photo_sides_fries_basket.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone snapshot of a basket of golden seasoned fries on a red diner tray, slightly tilted, flat grey lighting, cool cast, cluttered table edge visible. Real fried texture, salt flakes, slight grease, candid documentary feel.
inputs.realism_notes: Third hero crop; same imperfections so the four photos can be graded to a consistent appetizing look.

inputs.name: photo_bev_craft_cola.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone snapshot of a craft cola in a frosted glass bottle with condensation, on a wooden counter, dim warm bar light but slightly muddy/flat, busy shelf of bottles blurred behind. Real glass reflections, water droplets, no CGI.
inputs.realism_notes: Beverage hero; flat/muddy tone justifies the same grade and crop pipeline.

inputs.name: brickyard_wordmark.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat retro street-food wordmark logo reading 'Brickyard' in a bold condensed vintage diner typeface, chunky cream-white letters with a thin red outline, on a transparent background, slight retro distress. Crisp flat raster PNG, high contrast, no gradients, suitable for vectorizing.
inputs.realism_notes: gemini-3-pro-image for clean legible text/logo; flat high-contrast so image_vectorize produces a clean SVG.

inputs.name: menu_placeholder_prices.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a CSV with columns section,item,desc,price holding placeholder menu rows: 3 Classic Burgers, 3 Specialty Burgers, 4 Sides, 4 Beverages with short appetizing one-line descriptions and US-dollar placeholder prices (e.g. $7.50). Column names must exactly match the InDesign merge fields <<section>>,<<item>>,<<desc>>,<<price>>.
inputs.realism_notes: Honors the brief's 'just use placeholder text/prices' instruction; column headers match the authored .indd merge fields so the [T] data-merge binds.

outputs.name: Brickyard_Menu_print.pdf
outputs.spec: Print-ready one-page A4 portrait PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document; placeholder copy bound from CSV, graded food photos + vectorized wordmark + chalkboard texture placed; ~300 DPI print quality.

outputs.name: Brickyard_Menu_highres.jpg
outputs.spec: High-resolution JPEG of the same bound one-page menu (A4 portrait, max-quality) for digital/social sharing.

outputs.name: graded_food_photos_x4
outputs.spec: Four color-graded, straightened, consistently 4:5-cropped food photos (classic burger / specialty burger / fries / cola) matching the template frames; the classic hero also delivered as a transparent-background PNG.

outputs.name: brickyard_wordmark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vector SVG of the Brickyard wordmark for crisp scaling at large print size.

outputs.name: Brickyard_Menu_review_board
outputs.spec: Firefly board deep-link assembling the graded photos, vectorized wordmark, and final menu render for one-click owner sign-off.

connector_workflow.note: Search Adobe Stock for a rustic chalkboard/blackboard texture to back the menu (low-res candidates).
License and download the chosen chalkboard texture at full-res (license BEFORE use); becomes the layout background asset.
Level the tilted horizon on the hero double-smash shot.
Auto-tone the straightened hero to fix the flat/dull exposure.
Warm the cool white balance toward a hot street-food look.
Push vibrance so the cheese/bun/sauce read as appetizing.
Apply a food-pop Lightroom preset to lock a consistent appetizing look (same preset reused for the other three).
Throw the cluttered diner background out of focus so the burger reads as hero.
Crop/resize to the 4:5 frame the template expects (same target applied to all four photos).
Lift the finished hero burger onto a transparent background so it can sit over the chalkboard without a hard photo edge.
Run the specialty-burger photo through the same grade pipeline starting with auto-tone (fixes underexposure).
Warm the specialty shot to match the hero's white balance.
Blur the busy kitchen-pass background behind the specialty burger.
Crop the specialty photo to the matching 4:5 template frame.
Auto-tone the fries side shot to the consistent look.
Crop the fries photo to the 4:5 template frame.
Auto-tone the muddy/flat cola beverage shot.
Crop the cola photo to the 4:5 template frame so all four match.
Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG for razor-sharp large-print scaling.
Initialize an upload session for all prepped menu assets (graded photos, vector wordmark, texture).
Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs the InDesign template/data-merge will reference.

InDesign data-merge: bind the placeholder section/item/desc/price rows into the client's authored .indd (which carries real <<fields>>); prepped photos + texture + wordmark sit in their frames. [T] requires the user-authored template.
Export the merged InDesign document to a print-ready ~300 DPI A4 PDF deliverable.
Export the same merged document to a high-resolution JPEG deliverable.
Assemble the graded photos, vector wordmark and final menu render onto a Firefly board deep-link for owner sign-off.

chaining_note: Strict output→input chaining throughout. The hero photo runs a 7-link grade pipeline: photo_classic_double_smash.jpg → auto_straighten(3) → auto_tone(4) → color_temperature(5) → vibrance(6) → preset(7) → lens_blur(8) → crop(9) → remove_background(10), each step consuming only the prior step's output. The specialty/fries/cola photos each feed their own shorter grade→crop sub-chains (11-14, 15-16, 17-18). The chalkboard texture chains search(1)→license(2). The wordmark chains raw PNG→vectorize(19). All prepped outputs (classic_hero_transparent_png, specialty/fries/cola_cropped_4x5, wordmark_svg, chalkboard_texture_fullres) converge into the upload session(20)→finalize(21)→data-merge(22), which also consumes the authored menu_template.indd + prices CSV. The merged .indd(22) is the single source for both exports — render PDF(23) and render JPEG(24) — and four upstream outputs plus the JPEG converge into the Firefly review board(25). Nothing after step 1/2 (search) and the raw input loads originates outside the chain; the final menu emerges only from the assembled chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 25 connector calls across 4 tool groups (image_* raster, asset_* stock/storage, document_* InDesign, create_firefly_board), 16 distinct Adobe tools — far above the ≥10 calls / ≥6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running four separate photos through a consistent multi-step grade pipeline, branching the hero into a transparent cutout, vectorizing a logo, sourcing+licensing stock, then converging everything into a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge and dual export. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / object-removal / text-to-image / compositing / upscaling; image_fill_area is unused; the layout is NOT composed by the connector — render_layout/merge_data_layout only EXPORT/bind the client's authored .indd, which is listed as a supplied input; lens_blur is whole-image (no fake masked DOF claim); placeholder copy honored literally via CSV per the brief.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capabilities present. image_fill_area is not used. image_apply_lens_blur (n8, n13) is whole-image with no masked-DOF claim. image_remove_background (n10) produces a transparent PNG (not compositing/reconstruction). image_crop_and_resize handles all cropping (gen-free). document_merge_data_layout (n22) is [T] against a supplied user-authored .indd carrying real <<fields>>; document_render_layout (n23/n24) only exports. No generative-expand/outpaint, no upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image/from-scratch art, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no background-replace-by-prompt. asset_search is the Adobe stock/asset search (not the Express search_design widget). All 25 steps server=='adobe'. All step inputs are declared in inputs[] (authored .indd, CSV, four photos, wordmark PNG).
reverify.notes: All actions map to real Adobe connector tools and are gen-free within scope. The only edits were textual reconciliation of an over-claiming note/brief; the executable workflow (tools, exec_modes, chaining) is unchanged and remains fully doable.
